title: Han Dynasty Rule
summary: After defeating Au Lac, the Han Dynasty began a harsh ruling period, dividing our land into several districts.

slides.text: In 111 BC, the Han Dynasty invaded Au Lac. Our country was divided into districts: Giao Chi, Cuu Chan, and Nhat Nam.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient map of Vietnam divided into Han districts (Giao Chi, Cuu Chan, Nhat Nam), Han officials and soldiers, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Han colonial officials held all power. They imposed hundreds of heavy taxes, making our people's lives miserable.
slides.imagePrompt: A stern Han official collecting taxes (rice, precious goods) from poor Vietnamese villagers, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: They forced our people to provide rare products from the deep forests and the sea, such as pearls, ivory, and rhinoceros horns.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese people diving for pearls or hunting in dangerous forests to find products for invaders, risky and sad, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The brutal rule of the Han Dynasty ignited the fire of hatred and the will to regain independence among the Vietnamese people.
slides.imagePrompt: Villagers gathering in secret, looking determined and angry, preparing for a long struggle, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Into how many main districts did the Han Dynasty divide our country?
What was the main goal of imposing many different taxes?
options: To help the people become wealthy

slides.question: Which of the following products were our people forced to give to the invaders?
